key_highlights: New Fed Chair Warsh signals hawkish stance, triggering a sharp selloff in equities and a surge in bond yields, with the Dow logging its worst day in a week.
US-Iran deal takes effect, leading to a resumption of oil traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and a significant drop in oil prices, easing inflation fears but pressuring energy stocks.
SpaceX's post-IPO rally cools as retail frenzy subsides, but the company tops Amazon in market value and continues its massive AI fundraising campaign.
Bitcoin falls back toward $60,000 amid rate hike fears and strategy share concerns, while the broader crypto-treasury dream unravels after a 90% stock plunge.
Consumer relief emerges as gas prices fall below $4 for the first time since March, but a new poll shows Americans see a huge divide between haves and have-nots.
AI and cybersecurity remain bright spots, with Zscaler positioning to win big from AI agents and CoreWeave surging over 200% year-to-date.
Geopolitical tensions ease with the US-Iran deal, but critical oil storage hubs hit decades-low levels, and the UK gilt market tanks amid Labour civil war.
Apple's Tim Cook warns RAM expenses are 'unsustainable' and plans to raise prices, while Intel rallies on Trump-touted Apple chip deal.
Private credit redemptions at Oaktree drop below key 5% limit, signaling stress in alternative lending markets.
Bernie Sanders unveils plan for public ownership of AI companies, adding regulatory uncertainty to the sector.

sectors.trend: Mixed but with strong pockets. AI infrastructure and cybersecurity stocks are surging (CoreWeave +200% YTD, Zscaler bullish), while mega-cap tech faces valuation pressure from rising rates. Apple's price hike warning and Intel's chip deal with Trump highlight divergent paths.
sectors.implications: Investors should favor AI enablers and cybersecurity plays over consumer hardware exposed to rising costs. The Fed's hawkish stance may compress multiples for high-growth tech, but AI capex remains a secular tailwind.

sectors.trend: Sharp decline. Oil set for deep weekly loss as US-Iran deal restores Hormuz traffic, but critical storage hubs hit decades-low levels. TotalEnergies' Saudi refinery won't fully recover until 2027. Gas prices fall below $4 for first time since March.
sectors.implications: Short-term deflationary boost for consumers and transport, but energy infrastructure constraints remain. Oil stocks may be oversold; long-term supply tightness could re-emerge. Consider selective value plays in integrated majors with strong dividends.

sectors.trend: Bond yields surge on Fed rate-hike bets, dollar sees 'bullish break'. Schwab warns of margin calls on tax bets. Private credit redemptions at Oaktree drop below key 5% limit. Bank of England holds rates 7-2.
sectors.implications: Higher rates benefit banks' net interest margins but pressure leveraged borrowers and private credit. Margin call risk is rising. Defensive positioning in high-quality financials and short-duration bonds is prudent.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin falls back toward $60,000 amid rate fears and strategy share concerns. The crypto-treasury dream unravels after a 90% stock plunge. Hawkish Fed weighs on speculative assets.
sectors.implications: Crypto remains highly sensitive to liquidity conditions. The 'crypto-treasury' narrative is broken for now. Avoid speculative crypto-exposed equities; consider Bitcoin only as a small, long-term hedge with high volatility tolerance.

sectors.trend: SpaceX tops Amazon in market value but post-IPO rally cools. RTX stock carves out new price floor and is headed higher per chart analysis. SpaceX's AI fundraising campaign is just beginning.
sectors.implications: SpaceX's valuation reflects AI and space ambitions, but retail frenzy cooling suggests caution. RTX's technical setup is bullish. Defense spending remains a secular theme, but valuations are elevated.

sectors.trend: Mixed. Heart health biotech Kardigan up 38% after $400M IPO. Cabaletta Bio, Olema Pharmaceuticals, and Allogene Therapeutics show pipeline progress. ProKidney and Neumora have key catalysts. Illumina analyst report is neutral.
sectors.implications: Biotech remains a high-risk/high-reward space. Focus on companies with near-term catalysts (data readouts, FDA decisions) and strong balance sheets. IPO market is selectively open for innovative therapies.

sectors.trend: Pizza Hut sold for $2.7B, Papa John's closes 44 stores, showing fast-food struggles. Gas price relief boosts discretionary spending power. Amazon Prime Day deals early. 401(k) balances hit record levels, but wealth divide widens.
sectors.implications: Consumer is bifurcated: lower-income households feel inflation pain, while wealthier benefit from asset appreciation. Discount retailers and essential services may outperform. Pizza Hut's PE ownership signals consolidation in struggling brands.

sectors.trend: NRG Energy performance compared to utilities. Standard Nuclear files for US IPO. Deep Fission opens at $16/share as US turns to nuclear. Nuclear energy is gaining policy support as a clean baseload power source.
sectors.implications: Nuclear renaissance is a long-term theme. IPOs in this space offer early exposure but carry execution risk. Utilities with nuclear assets may benefit from AI data center power demand. Consider long-term positions in uranium and nuclear infrastructure.

key_themes: Hawkish Fed pivot under new Chair Warsh driving rate hike expectations and bond selloff
US-Iran deal deflating oil prices but exposing infrastructure fragility
AI infrastructure and cybersecurity as the only clear growth pockets amid macro uncertainty
Crypto and speculative assets under pressure from rising real yields
Consumer bifurcation: gas price relief vs. widening wealth inequality
Private credit and margin call risks rising as liquidity tightens
Geopolitical realignment: US-Iran detente, UK gilt crisis, Indonesia market information concerns
Regulatory overhang: AI public ownership proposal, AI chip tracking mandate, pollution lawsuits
Nuclear energy renaissance as a bipartisan solution for AI power needs
SpaceX's dual narrative: AI fundraising juggernaut vs. cooling retail enthusiasm

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios, favor floating-rate debt, increase cash allocation. Hedge equity exposure with put spreads or inverse ETFs.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil Supply Disruption Despite Iran Deal
risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain strategic energy exposure (integrated majors, midstream) as a hedge. Critical storage at decades-low levels means any new disruption could spike prices.
Avoid leveraged credit strategies. Monitor Oaktree redemption trends and Schwab margin call warnings. Reduce exposure to BDC and private credit funds.
Limit crypto exposure to less than 2% of portfolio. Avoid crypto-exposed equities. The 'crypto-treasury' narrative is broken; wait for regulatory clarity.
Diversify geographically. UK gilt exposure is risky due to Labour civil war. Indonesia market information flow is worsening. US political uncertainty around AI regulation and billionaire tax.
Monitor Sanders' AI public ownership proposal and chip tracking mandate. Favor AI companies with diversified revenue and strong lobbying power. Avoid single-point AI plays.
Focus on defensive consumer staples and discount retailers. Public-school staff layoffs signal state budget stress. Wealth divide may lead to social unrest.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate AI infrastructure and cybersecurity leaders on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: CoreWeave, Zscaler, and Broadcom are benefiting from secular AI capex. Despite macro headwinds, AI spending remains a priority for enterprises and governments. KeyBanc sees more room for CoreWeave.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy oil majors on weakness for income and supply tightness hedge
opportunities.rationale: Oil's sharp drop is overdone given critical storage lows and Saudi refinery delays. TotalEnergies and SLB offer attractive yields and digital growth. SLB targets doubling digital revenue by 2030.

opportunities.recommendation: Selectively add to nuclear and utility stocks for AI power demand
opportunities.rationale: Standard Nuclear and Deep Fission IPOs signal growing investor interest. Nuclear provides clean baseload power for AI data centers. NRG Energy and other utilities with nuclear assets are well-positioned.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider short-duration Treasuries and floating-rate notes
opportunities.rationale: With the Fed likely to hike again in July, short-duration bonds offer attractive yields with less price risk. Floating-rate notes benefit from rising rates. Dollar strength supports USD-denominated assets.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy beaten-down biotech with near-term catalysts
opportunities.rationale: Cabaleta Bio, Olema Pharmaceuticals, and Neumora have key data readouts in autoimmune and oncology. Risk/reward is favorable for binary events. Use small position sizes.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation to 15-20% of portfolio
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising rate volatility, margin call risks, and geopolitical uncertainty warrant higher cash reserves. Cash provides optionality to deploy during drawdowns.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge equity portfolios with put spreads on S&P 500 or QQQ
defensive_moves.rationale: The Dow's worst day in a week and surging bond yields suggest further downside risk. A put spread on SPY or QQQ can protect against a 5-10% correction at reasonable cost.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to private credit and leveraged loans
defensive_moves.rationale: Oaktree redemptions dropping below 5% limit and Schwab margin call warnings signal stress in credit markets. Liquidity is tightening. Move to high-quality corporate bonds or Treasuries.
Bitcoin falling toward $60,000 and the crypto-treasury dream unraveling (90% stock plunge) indicate a broken narrative. Hawkish Fed is toxic for speculative assets. Stay away.
The Great Gilt Gamble: UK bonds tank as Labour civil war heats up. Political instability and fiscal concerns make UK assets unattractive. Rotate to US or Asian markets.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish bias with high volatility. The Fed under Warsh is firmly hawkish, with July rate hike wagers surging. Bond yields will continue to pressure equity valuations, especially in growth and tech. Oil's decline provides some inflation relief but infrastructure risks remain. The US-Iran deal is a positive geopolitical development but may be temporary. Expect further downside in risk assets, with the S&P 500 testing key support levels. Defensive sectors (utilities, healthcare, consumer staples) may outperform. Crypto and speculative stocks face headwinds.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously optimistic. The Fed's hawkish stance should eventually bring inflation under control, setting the stage for rate cuts in late 2026 or early 2027. AI infrastructure spending remains a powerful secular trend. Nuclear energy and cybersecurity are long-term winners. The wealth divide and political polarization are structural risks but may drive policy responses. Oil supply tightness could re-emerge as a bullish catalyst. Diversification across quality growth, income, and defensive assets is key.
key_catalysts: July FOMC meeting and rate decision (rate hike probability rising)
US-Iran deal implementation and oil traffic normalization
AI earnings season: Nvidia, CoreWeave, Broadcom reports
US consumer price index (CPI) and employment data for June
UK Labour civil war outcome and gilt market stability
SpaceX AI fundraising campaign details and valuation
Bernie Sanders' AI public ownership bill progress
Critical oil storage levels and any supply disruption
Geopolitical developments: Indonesia market access, US-China chip tensions
watch_list: 10-year US Treasury yield (key resistance at 4.50% and 5.00%)
WTI crude oil price and storage levels at Cushing, OK
Dollar index (DXY) and its impact on emerging markets
Margin debt levels and broker warnings (Schwab, Robinhood)
UK 10-year gilt yield spread over Bunds
AI chip tracking mandate legislative progress
Pizza Hut/Papa John's store closure impact on restaurant REITs
Kardigan IPO aftermarket performance as biotech sentiment indicator
